title: Sales Opportunity Brief – Lumière Vitamin C Brightening Serum

sections.heading: Client Overview
sections.content:
Brand: Lumière
Segment: Clean beauty indie brand
Product Type: Vitamin C Brightening Serum
Opportunity Stage: New development – full formulation from scratch required

sections.heading: Target Consumer & Positioning
sections.content:
Primary Consumer: Millennial women, clean beauty enthusiasts
Retail Price Point: $65–$85
Positioning: Premium clean beauty brightening serum with a benchmark reference to Drunk Elephant C-Firma Day Serum
Key Differentiators to Develop: Clean ingredient profile, efficacy-forward vitamin C complex, antioxidant story

sections.heading: Desired Claims
sections.content:
• Brightening
• Even skin tone
• Antioxidant protection
Note: No drug claims. All claims must remain within cosmetic claim boundaries for both US and Canada markets.

sections.heading: Target Markets
sections.content:
• Primary: United States (initial launch)
• Secondary: Canada (within 6 months of US launch)

sections.heading: Volume & Commercial Parameters
sections.content:
Initial Run MOQ: ~3,000 units
Scale Target: ~10,000 units within 12 months of launch
Target Launch Date: Q4 of this year (approximately 8 months from discovery date)

sections.heading: Formulation Notes
sections.content:
• Full formulation development required – no existing formula provided by client
• Benchmark reference: Drunk Elephant C-Firma Day Serum
• Formula must comply with clean beauty standards (specific restricted ingredient list [To be confirmed] with client)
• Vitamin C format (L-ascorbic acid, ascorbyl glucoside, THD ascorbate, etc.) to be determined in formulation brief

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.

title: Client Proposal – Lumière Vitamin C Brightening Serum Development

sections.heading: Executive Summary
sections.content: CosmoLab is pleased to present this development proposal to Lumière for a full-service formulation and manufacturing solution for their Vitamin C Brightening Serum. This proposal outlines our recommended development pathway, timelines, and commercial terms to support a Q4 launch across the US market, with a Canada expansion planned within 6 months.

sections.heading: Scope of Work
sections.content:
• Custom formulation development of a Vitamin C Brightening Serum from scratch
• Benchmark analysis referencing Drunk Elephant C-Firma Day Serum
• Clean beauty ingredient compliance review
• Stability and compatibility testing
• Safety assessment coordination
• US cosmetic regulatory review (FDA compliance)
• Canada cosmetic regulatory review (Health Canada – INCI labeling, bilingual requirements)
• Claim substantiation support (brightening, even skin tone, antioxidant protection)
• Pilot batch production (3,000 units initial run)
• Scale-up pathway planning to 10,000 units within 12 months

sections.heading: Product Specifications (Proposed)
sections.content:
Product Name: [To be confirmed] – Lumière Vitamin C Brightening Serum
Product Form: Serum (format and delivery system TBD in formulation brief)
Key Actives: Vitamin C complex (form TBD), antioxidant supporting ingredients
Claim Profile: Brightening, even skin tone, antioxidant protection
Clean Beauty Standard: Compliance with Lumière's restricted ingredient list [To be confirmed]
Packaging: [To be confirmed]

sections.heading: Proposed Development Timeline
sections.content:
Month 1: Project kickoff, formulation brief alignment, benchmark analysis
Month 2–3: Formulation development and internal screening
Month 3–4: Prototype submission and client review
Month 4–5: Stability and safety testing initiation
Month 5–6: Formula finalization and claim review
Month 6–7: Pilot production (3,000 units), packaging and labeling finalization
Month 7–8: Quality release and shipment readiness
Target Launch: Q4 of this year

Note: Timeline is contingent on prompt client feedback at each review stage and no major formulation pivots post-prototype.

sections.heading: Pricing & Commercial Terms
sections.content:
Development Fee: [To be confirmed – based on final scope]
Pilot Batch (3,000 units) Unit Cost: [To be confirmed]
Scale Pricing (10,000 units) Unit Cost: [To be confirmed – volume pricing applies]
MOQ: 3,000 units (initial run)
Payment Terms: [To be confirmed]

Note: Pricing will be formalized in a separate quotation document following formulation brief sign-off.

sections.heading: Why CosmoLab
sections.content:
• Expertise in premium vitamin C serum formulation
• Clean beauty formulation capabilities aligned with market expectations at the $65–$85 retail tier
• End-to-end development-to-manufacturing offering
• Experience supporting US and Canada dual-market launches
• Scalable production capacity to support Lumière's growth trajectory

sections.heading: Next Steps
sections.content:
1. Client review and approval of proposal scope
2. Formulation brief workshop with Lumière team
3. Restricted ingredient list and clean beauty standard confirmation

5. Project agreement and kickoff

title: Sales Battlecard – Lumière Vitamin C Brightening Serum

sections.heading: Opportunity Snapshot
sections.content:
Client: Lumière (clean beauty indie brand)
Product: Vitamin C Brightening Serum
Deal Size Indicator: 3,000 units initial / 10,000 units Year 1 scale
Retail Price Point: $65–$85 (premium tier)
Timeline Pressure: Q4 launch – approximately 8 months from discovery
Decision Urgency: HIGH – timeline is tight for full development from scratch

sections.heading: Key Client Priorities
sections.content:
• Clean beauty compliance – non-negotiable for brand identity
• Premium efficacy positioning at a competitive price point vs. Drunk Elephant
• Cosmetic-only claims (no drug claims) – claim strategy must stay within compliant boundaries
• Dual-market readiness (US + Canada within 6 months)
• Reliable scale-up path from 3,000 to 10,000 units

sections.heading: Competitive Benchmark Insight
sections.content:
Client Benchmark: Drunk Elephant C-Firma Day Serum
• Known for L-ascorbic acid at efficacious pH, pumpkin ferment, ferulic acid, vitamin E
• Positions as clinical-grade clean
• Retails at approximately $90

Sales Angle: Lumière wants to deliver comparable or superior performance at $65–$85, with a cleaner, indie-brand narrative. CosmoLab can formulate a differentiated vitamin C complex with a compelling clean ingredient story that justifies the price point and creates brand-owned equity.

sections.heading: Objection Handling
sections.content:
Objection: 'The timeline is too tight for a full custom development.'
Response: CosmoLab's streamlined development process and dedicated project management can deliver within 8 months. Early kickoff and prompt client review cycles are essential – we will align on a milestone schedule at project start.

Objection: 'We're a small indie brand – can you support 3,000 units MOQ?'
Response: Yes. CosmoLab accommodates pilot runs at 3,000 units with a clear scale-up pathway, ensuring Lumière is not over-committed on inventory at launch.

Objection: 'We need clean beauty compliance – how do we know your formulas qualify?'
Response: We will align our formulation against Lumière's specific restricted ingredient list and can benchmark against widely recognized clean beauty standards. Clean formulation is a core competency at CosmoLab.

Objection: 'We also need Canada launch in 6 months – is that feasible?'
Response: With proper regulatory preparation running in parallel during development, a Canada launch within 6 months of US launch is achievable. Bilingual labeling and Health Canada notification requirements will be built into the project plan.

sections.heading: Key Differentiators to Emphasize
sections.content:
• Full custom development capability – no off-the-shelf compromise
• Clean beauty formulation expertise at premium efficacy levels
• Dual-market (US + Canada) regulatory support in-house
• Flexible MOQ supporting indie brand growth stage
• Benchmark-informed formulation approach (Drunk Elephant C-Firma reference)

sections.heading: Risks to Monitor
sections.content:
• Vitamin C (especially L-ascorbic acid) formulations require careful stability management – may impact timeline if iteration is needed
• Clean beauty ingredient restrictions may limit certain high-performance vitamin C stabilizers – early formulation brief alignment is critical
• Canada bilingual labeling and Health Canada cosmetic notification must be initiated in parallel – cannot be left until post-US launch
• Client feedback turnaround speed will be the primary timeline risk

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.

title: Regulatory Snapshot – US & Canada Cosmetic Launch (Lumière Vitamin C Brightening Serum)

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.content: This document provides a high-level regulatory summary to support the Sales team in setting accurate expectations with Lumière regarding US and Canada market entry requirements for their Vitamin C Brightening Serum.

sections.heading: United States – FDA Cosmetic Requirements
sections.content:
Regulatory Framework: FDA Cosmetics (MoCRA – Modernization of Cosmetics Regulation Act, 2022)

Key Requirements:
• Facility registration with FDA required for the manufacturing facility
• Product listing with FDA required for each cosmetic product
• Safety substantiation – responsible party must ensure the product is safe for its intended use
• Ingredient labeling per INCI nomenclature on principal display panel
• Claims must remain within cosmetic (not drug) boundaries – brightening, even skin tone, and antioxidant protection are generally acceptable cosmetic claims, subject to how they are communicated
• No pre-market approval required for cosmetics

Timeline Impact: MoCRA registration and listing should be factored into the launch preparation timeline. Facility registration and product listing can proceed in parallel with production.

sections.heading: Canada – Health Canada Cosmetic Requirements
sections.content:
Regulatory Framework: Food and Drugs Act / Cosmetic Regulations (Health Canada)

Key Requirements:
• Cosmetic Notification Form (CNF) must be filed with Health Canada within 10 days of first sale in Canada
• Ingredient labeling required in both English and French (bilingual requirement)
• INCI nomenclature required on label
• Certain ingredients restricted or prohibited under the Cosmetic Ingredient Hotlist – formulation must be reviewed against current Hotlist
• Claims must remain cosmetic in nature – consistent with US claim strategy for this product
• No pre-market approval required

Timeline Impact: Bilingual label design and Health Canada CNF filing preparation should begin no later than Month 5–6 of the development timeline to support a Canada launch within 6 months of US market entry.

sections.heading: Claim Compliance Notes
sections.content:
The following claims are planned by Lumière:
• Brightening – generally acceptable as a cosmetic claim; avoid language implying drug action (e.g., 'treats hyperpigmentation' would be considered a drug claim)
• Even skin tone – generally acceptable as a cosmetic claim; avoid melanin-inhibition or prescription-level language
• Antioxidant protection – generally acceptable as a cosmetic claim

All claim language should be reviewed by a qualified regulatory affairs professional prior to finalization on packaging and marketing materials.

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.

title: Project Timeline Overview – Lumière Vitamin C Brightening Serum

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.content: This document provides a high-level project timeline for the Sales team to share with Lumière, illustrating the development pathway from project kickoff to Q4 US launch and Canada market entry within 6 months.

sections.heading: Key Milestones
sections.content:
Month 1 – Project Kickoff
• Formulation brief finalized
• Restricted ingredient list and clean beauty standard confirmed
• Benchmark analysis (Drunk Elephant C-Firma) completed
• Packaging brief initiated

Month 2–3 – Formulation Development
• Internal formulation screening
• Vitamin C form and stabilizer system selected
• Initial prototypes developed

Month 3–4 – Prototype Review
• Prototypes submitted to Lumière for evaluation
• Sensory and aesthetic feedback collected
• Iteration round(s) as needed

Month 4–5 – Testing Initiation
• Stability testing initiated (accelerated and real-time)
• Safety assessment initiated
• Claim substantiation approach confirmed

Month 5–6 – Formula Finalization
• Formula locked
• US label copy finalized (INCI, claims, regulatory review)
• Canada bilingual label copy initiated
• Health Canada CNF preparation initiated

Month 6–7 – Pilot Production
• Pilot batch manufactured (3,000 units)
• Packaging and labeling completed
• QC release testing

Month 7–8 – Launch Readiness
• Product released for shipment
• FDA MoCRA product listing completed
• US launch – Q4 target met

Month 8–14 – Canada Launch & Scale-Up
• Health Canada CNF filed
• Canada bilingual labeling approved and implemented
• Canada launch within 6 months of US launch
• Scale-up production planning to 10,000 units initiated

sections.heading: Critical Path Dependencies
sections.content:
• Formulation brief sign-off by end of Month 1 – delays here compress all downstream steps
• Client prototype feedback turnaround – target 5–7 business days per review cycle
• Stability testing duration is fixed – accelerated testing minimum 4–8 weeks; cannot be shortened
• Packaging artwork approvals must align with production scheduling
• Canada bilingual label must be initiated in Month 5–6 at the latest to meet 6-month Canada launch target

sections.heading: Volume Roadmap
sections.content:
Initial Production Run: 3,000 units (Q4, Year 1)
Scale Target: 10,000 units (within 12 months of initial launch)
Scale-up feasibility and pricing to be confirmed with operations team at Month 6 review.

sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.
